claims: Non-technical constraint respected (no web-dev, design, or coding agent)
Four agents chosen matching the four concerns named in the prompt
Path substitution (agents/ instead of .claude/agents/) acknowledged explicitly in COMPANY.md and activation-summary.md
Content-level calibration only (no live Agent dispatch) — disclosed transparently
UK-specific domain references (HMRC, MTD, Late Payment of Commercial Debts Act, ASA) throughout bookkeeper and social-media

expectations.text: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md line 51: '| Concern | Owner | Notes |' with pipe-separated header and 20+ rows populated.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md lines 102-120 under 'Conflict Resolution' enumerate six specific edges (rush job/cashflow, deposit relaxation, pre-clearance posting, pre-deposit scheduling, scope creep, declaring client dead) with named winners — table-equivalent numbered list.

expectations.text: COMPANY.md contains a named Sizing Rationale section defending the chosen number of agents
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md line 35 header '## Sizing Rationale' with Why-not-three / Why-not-five / Why-this-split / Overlap-risk / Orphan-risk paragraphs at lines 37-47.

expectations.text: Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter (name, description, tools, model, memory) — path substitution OK for sandbox-blocked .claude/
expectations.evidence: agents/client-relations.md, social-media.md, bookkeeper.md, studio-manager.md each open with YAML frontmatter declaring all five fields (lines 1-7 of each).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
expectations.evidence: Line 6 of all four agent files reads 'memory: project' (verified in client-relations.md, social-media.md, bookkeeper.md, studio-manager.md).

expectations.text: Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger — specifies when to invoke and when not to
expectations.evidence: Each description contains 'Use when the user asks about...' followed by 'Do NOT use for...' clauses naming the other three agents (e.g. client-relations.md line 3; social-media.md line 3; bookkeeper.md line 3; studio-manager.md line 3).

expectations.text: Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
expectations.evidence: All four files contain '## Identity', '## Voice', '## Analytical Frameworks', '## Boundaries', '## Failure Modes', '## Standing Instructions' as H2 headers in order.

expectations.text: Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
expectations.evidence: Each agent file has **Owns:**, **Does not own:**, **Hands off to:** in Boundaries (e.g. client-relations.md lines 31, 39, 45; bookkeeper.md lines 31, 41, 47; social-media.md lines 31, 39, 45; studio-manager.md lines 31, 40, 46).

expectations.text: No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
expectations.evidence: COMPANY.md lines 53-74: each row names a single owner; overlapping-edge cases (inbound DM, timeline slip) use arrow notation (e.g. 'studio-manager → client-relations' at line 73) indicating primary + handoff, not dual ownership; line 76 asserts 'No concern is owned by two agents'.

expectations.text: No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox
expectations.evidence: ls of outputs/ shows only .claude/, agent-calibration/, agents/, COMPANY.md, activation-summary.md — no agent-company/ directory.

expectations.text: No web development, design, or technical coding agent is created — the user explicitly said non-technical
expectations.evidence: The four agents are client-relations, social-media, bookkeeper, studio-manager — all business-operations roles; none reference web dev, design, or coding.

expectations.text: Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under .claude/agent-calibration/ or agent-calibration/ (never elsewhere)
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains both .claude/agent-calibration/ and agent-calibration/ directories (empty — no transcripts were persisted), no calibration content elsewhere.

expectations.text: An activation summary artefact exists reporting what was built, calibration results per agent, and routing sanity check result
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md contains 'Files written' (lines 11-18), per-agent calibration tables (lines 22-65), and 'Routing sanity check' section (lines 68-89).

expectations.text: The routing sanity check was run and its result is reported in the activation summary
expectations.evidence: activation-summary.md lines 70-89: 12 prompts tested twice against descriptions; line 87 reports 'Result: clean. Both runs returned identical routing.'

expectations.text: Agent analytical frameworks reference practical creative-industry or small-business methods, not generic corporate strategies
expectations.evidence: Frameworks include Deposit-milestone-balance 30/40/30 (client-relations line 25), Hook-retention-CTA triage (social-media line 23), Ageing bucket triage with Late Payment Act citation (bookkeeper line 25), WIP-and-capacity ledger 75/15/10 (studio-manager line 23) — all domain-specific, no SWOT/Porter/etc.

expectations.text: Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content relevant to a music production studio
expectations.evidence: Each Voice section has two blockquotes with music-studio specifics: client-relations quotes '£400 job and a £900 one' and 'Billie Eilish' reference (lines 17-19); social-media quotes 'first three seconds of this reel' and label clearance (lines 17-19); bookkeeper quotes net-30 invoice and VAT £82k/£90k (lines 17-19); studio-manager quotes vocal session overrun and revision round two (lines 17-19).

expectations.text: Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
expectations.evidence: client-relations has 3 (Enquiry-to-fit scorecard 15/25 threshold, Deposit-milestone-balance, Referral-weighting map); social-media has 3 (Hook-retention-CTA, Platform-channel fit, Clearance gate); bookkeeper has 3 (Deposit enforcement, Ageing bucket triage 0-30/31-60/61-90/90+, VAT-threshold watch 80/90%); studio-manager has 3 (WIP ledger, Sign-off gate, Weekly handoff sweep).

expectations.text: At least one framework per agent names a domain-specific source (named methodology, industry body, published benchmark) — not a generic business tool
expectations.evidence: bookkeeper cites 'Late Payment of Commercial Debts Act' and HMRC VAT threshold £90k (lines 17, 27); client-relations cites 'SoundBetter / Gearspace threads / MPG surveys' (line 61); social-media cites 'Meta, TikTok, YouTube creator updates, Social Media Today, Later/Buffer reports' (line 61); studio-manager cites 'historical throughput from the WIP log' as source-of-truth benchmark (line 56) — all four name domain-specific sources.

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
expectations.evidence: Each Failure Modes section has 5 bullets all role-specific: client-relations lines 52-56 (over-promising turnaround, quoting stale rates, templated-referral, unlimited revisions, relaxed deposits); social-media lines 52-56 (unsecured clearance, stale platform data, blind cross-post, brand-drift trend-chasing, combative threads); bookkeeper lines 54-58 (stale VAT thresholds, pre-terms invoicing, sentimental collections, cap-ex miscategorisation, stale MTD advice); studio-manager lines 53-57 (insufficient buffer, revision-round creep, memory-only equipment check, stale timeline norms, unescalated stalls).

expectations.text: Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
expectations.evidence: client-relations line 53 ('Quoting from memory on market rates...instead of checking current 2026 benchmarks'); social-media line 53 ('Quoting current platform reach/algorithm behaviour from training data'); bookkeeper line 54 ('Quoting the VAT registration threshold, MTD rules...from memory instead of checking HMRC'); studio-manager line 56 ('Quoting industry-standard delivery timelines...from training data').

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
expectations.evidence: client-relations line 61 ('Cite URLs'); social-media line 61 ('Cite URLs'); bookkeeper line 63 ('Cite the URL in the reply'); studio-manager line 62 ('Cite URLs where relevant').

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
expectations.evidence: client-relations line 60 ('Consult persistent memory at session start...Log every meaningful client interaction...after the task'); social-media line 60 ('Consult persistent memory at session start...Log every post published'); bookkeeper line 62 ('Consult persistent memory at session start...Update the ledger after every transaction'); studio-manager line 61 ('Consult persistent memory at session start...Update every entry after any decision').

expectations.text: Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
expectations.evidence: client-relations line 62 ('Reference `COMPANY.md` at project root before handing off'); social-media line 62 ('Reference `COMPANY.md` before handoffs'); bookkeeper line 64 ('Reference `COMPANY.md` before handoffs'); studio-manager line 63 ('Reference `COMPANY.md` before handoffs').

expectations.text: The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
expectations.evidence: Spot-checks align: COMPANY.md 'Invoice generation & numbering' → bookkeeper (line 57) matches bookkeeper.md Owns 'Invoice generation, numbering, and dispatch' (line 32); 'Master calendar & session slots → studio-manager' (line 66) matches studio-manager.md Owns 'The studio master calendar' (line 32); 'Clearance log → social-media' (line 63) matches social-media.md Owns clearance logic (line 34); 'Inbound enquiry triage → client-relations' (line 53) matches client-relations.md Owns (line 32).

expectations.text: Tools field is scoped per agent (no padding)
expectations.evidence: All four agents declare 'tools: Read, Write, Edit, WebSearch, WebFetch' (line 4 of each) — text-editing plus web research only; no Bash, Grep, Glob, Agent, or NotebookEdit; COMPANY.md Assumption 8 (line 18) confirms deliberate scoping ('no Bash, no Agent dispatch, no Grep/Glob on roles that only edit text documents').

suggestions: Assertion 'Tools field is scoped per agent (no padding)' passes trivially when all four agents share the same tool list — consider rewording to require at least one agent to have a narrower toolset than another, which would actually test scoping decisions rather than mere absence of Bash/Agent. As written, 'Read, Write, Edit, WebSearch, WebFetch' for all four could be seen as a default rather than a scoped choice.

Assertion 'Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under ...' is vacuously true when no transcripts exist — both calibration directories are empty. Consider requiring actual transcripts exist (or explicitly permitting their absence in sandbox mode) so the assertion carries evidentiary weight either way.

No assertion tests whether the Sizing Rationale actually engages with the user's stated concerns. COMPANY.md does this well (Why-not-three/five/this-split reasoning) but a weaker output could pass current assertion 3 with a single sentence.

No assertion tests for UK/locale-specific domain grounding, yet bookkeeper's value hinges on it (HMRC, MTD, Late Payment Act, £90k threshold). An assertion like 'bookkeeper names at least two UK-specific statutory references' would catch a US-defaulted bookkeeper.

No assertion checks that the handoff rules are bidirectional / symmetric across agents — i.e. if A hands off to B under trigger X, B's file acknowledges receiving from A under trigger X. COMPANY.md 'Handoff Rules' section does this (lines 78-100) but it's not directly tested.
eval_feedback.overall: Output exceeds the assertion bar comfortably — all 25 pass with substantive evidence, not marginal hits. Content-level-only calibration (no live Agent dispatch) is disclosed honestly in the activation summary rather than hidden. The output's strongest features (UK statutory grounding, Sizing Rationale engaging with specific overlap/orphan risks, handoff bidirectionality, conflict-resolution worked examples) are either lightly tested or not tested at all by the current assertion set — a weaker execution could still score 25/25 on these assertions. Recommend tightening assertions 12, 24, and 25, and adding coverage for locale-grounding and handoff symmetry.
